rationale: Title Y follows JACC's declarative subtitle convention, names the intervention precisely, and packs the key finding plus study design and follow-up duration into a clean, unambiguous structure, whereas Title X's question format and informal phrasing are atypical for the journal.
loser_weakness: Title X uses an informal question-format headline uncommon in JACC, omits follow-up duration, and the phrase 'Harm Cognition' is grammatically imprecise for a clinical audience.
